analysis_metadata.specification: Matter Core Specification 1.5, Section 11.25 Joint Fabric Administrator Cluster

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM enforces CASE session authentication through guard conditions in T001 and explicit rejection in T002
FSM enforces CASE session authentication through guard conditions in T007 and explicit rejection in T008
FSM requires Crypto_VerifyChain success in T011 guard and rejects failures in T012
FSM validates public key match in T011 guard and rejects mismatches in T013
FSM enforces fail-safe requirement through guard in T001 and explicit rejection in T003
FSM enforces fail-safe requirement through guard in T007 and explicit rejection in T009
FSM requires VID verification in T001 guard and rejects unverified peers in T004
FSM enforces single AddICAC through prior_AddICAC_executed guards in T001, T005, T007, T010
FSM requires DN encoding validation in T011 guard and rejects failures in T014
FSM correctly implements two-phase commit: DeletePending -> {Removed | CommitFailed} in T024, T027, T028
T048 accepts AnnounceJointFabricAdministrator without validating endpoint uniqueness or JFAC cluster presence
FSM validates PAKE parameters (verifier, iterations, salt) through validate_pake_parameters function in T031
FSM implicitly prevents concurrent operations through state design but lacks explicit Busy rejection for concurrent ICACCSRRequest attempts
validate_pake_parameters function does not include discriminator validation; values >4095 accepted
validate_pake_parameters correctly validates iterations in range [1000, 100000]
validate_pake_parameters correctly validates salt length in range [16, 32] bytes
Single-threaded FSM state progression enforces implicit session binding; public key validation in T011-T013 prevents substitution
No fail-safe expiration transitions from CSRRequested, CSRGenerated, or ValidatingICAC states; partial state persists beyond fail-safe period
Four state machines (Joint Commissioning, ACL Management, Commissioning Window, Anchor Transfer) can run concurrently without mutual exclusion
No size validation when generating or sending CSR; values >600 bytes could be sent
No size validation when receiving ICAC in AddICAC; values >400 bytes could be accepted

attack_path.guard: session_is_CASE == true && fail_safe_armed == true && vid_verification_completed == true

attack_path.violation: State persists with fail-safe expired; no rollback transition
CSR and public key stored but fail-safe expired; partial state persists
Validation in progress but fail-safe expired; ICAC partially processed

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: FSM only models fail-safe expiration rollback from ICACAccepted state (T016). Intermediate states CSRRequested, CSRGenerated, and ValidatingICAC have no fail-safe expiration transitions. If fail-safe expires in these states, node remains in intermediate state with partial commissioning data.

specification_evidence.quote: If this command is received without an armed fail-safe context (see Section 11.10.7.2, "ArmFailSafe"), then this command SHALL fail with a FAILSAFE_REQUIRED status code sent back to the initiator.
specification_evidence.source: Section 11.25.7.1, ICACCSRRequest Command, Effect on Receipt, Paragraph 2
specification_evidence.context: Mandates fail-safe context for ICACCSRRequest command
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Spec requires fail-safe but doesn't specify rollback behavior for intermediate states when fail-safe expires

specification_evidence.quote: If this command is received without an armed fail-safe context (see Section 11.10.7.2, "ArmFailSafe"), then this command SHALL fail with a FAILSAFE_REQUIRED status code sent back to the initiator.
specification_evidence.source: Section 11.25.7.3, AddICAC Command, Effect on Receipt, Paragraph 2
specification_evidence.context: Mandates fail-safe context for AddICAC command
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Spec requires fail-safe protection for entire commissioning flow

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Commands must be received with armed fail-safe context
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: What happens when fail-safe expires during intermediate states (after CSR generated but before commissioning complete). Specification only implies fail-safe protection but doesn't explicitly mandate atomic rollback from all states.
specification_gap.consequence: Implementations may leave node in partial commissioning state when fail-safe expires, with CSR public key stored, credentials partially installed, or validation in progress
specification_gap.security_impact: Node left in inconsistent state; CSR public key accessible; partial credentials cannot be recovered or rolled back; subsequent commissioning attempts fail due to polluted state; DoS through state exhaustion

steps: 1. Administrator initiates joint commissioning with ArmFailSafe(timeout=60s)
2. Administrator sends ICACCSRRequest over CASE
3. Node transitions: Idle -> CSRRequested (T001)
4. Node generates CSR and stores csr_public_key
5. Node transitions: CSRRequested -> CSRGenerated (T006)
6. Node sends ICACCSRResponse to Administrator
7. Network partition or Administrator crash: AddICAC never sent
8. Fail-safe timer expires (60 seconds elapsed)
9. VIOLATION: Node remains in CSRGenerated state
10. Stored state: csr_public_key persists, fail_safe_armed = false
11. Subsequent ICACCSRRequest attempts fail (FAILSAFE_REQUIRED)
12. Subsequent ArmFailSafe attempts check if commissioning in progress
13. Node in undefined state: not commissioning, not idle, CSR data present
counterexample_scenario.impact: Denial of Service - node cannot be re-commissioned without factory reset; partial credential data persists in memory; security state machine stuck

recommendations: Add transition T00X: CSRRequested -> Idle on fail_safe_expired trigger with action: reset_commissioning_state()
Add transition T00Y: CSRGenerated -> Idle on fail_safe_expired trigger with action: rollback_all_operations(); clear_csr_data()
Add transition T00Z: ValidatingICAC -> Idle on fail_safe_expired trigger with action: rollback_all_operations(); clear_csr_data(); clear_icac_data()
Specification should mandate: 'When fail-safe expires, all commissioning state SHALL be atomically rolled back regardless of current state'
Implement watchdog: fail-safe expiration automatically triggers cleanup from ANY commissioning state

attack_path.guard: AdministratorFabricIndex != null && validate_pake_parameters(verifier, iterations, salt) == success
attack_path.violation: Discriminator >4095 accepted; validate_pake_parameters only checks verifier, iterations, salt

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: OpenJointCommissioningWindow has 5 parameters: CommissioningTimeout, PAKEPasscodeVerifier, Discriminator, Iterations, Salt. FSM's validate_pake_parameters function only validates 3: verifier (97 bytes), iterations (1000-100000), salt (16-32 bytes). Discriminator validation is missing. Specification defines 'Discriminator ... uint16 ... max 4095' but FSM doesn't enforce constraint.

specification_evidence.source: Section 11.25.7.5, OpenJointCommissioningWindow Command, Parameter Table, Row 2
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Specification clearly defines max value but FSM doesn't validate

specification_evidence.quote:
PAKEPasscodeVerifier ... octstr ... 97
Iterations ... uint32 ... 1000 to 100000
Salt ... octstr ... 16 to 32
specification_evidence.context: Other parameter constraints that ARE validated by FSM
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: FSM validates some parameters but not discriminator

specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: Whether validation should occur on sender or receiver side. Specification defines constraint but doesn't mandate validation in 'Effect on Receipt' section (which doesn't exist for OpenJointCommissioningWindow).
specification_gap.consequence: Invalid discriminator values accepted, potentially causing discovery collisions (discriminator used for BLE advertisement), integer overflow if cast to 12-bit field, or probing of validation logic
specification_gap.security_impact: Discovery collision - multiple devices advertise same discriminator; integer overflow in 12-bit discriminator field implementation; commissioning window becomes non-discoverable or ambiguous

steps: 1. Malicious Administrator sends OpenJointCommissioningWindow
2. Parameters: discriminator=65535 (0xFFFF), valid verifier/iterations/salt
3. FSM validates PAKE parameters (verifier, iterations, salt) ✅
4. FSM checks AdministratorFabricIndex != null ✅
5. All guards pass in T031
6. Node transitions: CommissioningWindow_Closed -> CommissioningWindow_Open
7. Commissioning window opens with discriminator=65535
8. VIOLATION: discriminator exceeds max 4095
9. Impact scenarios:
a. BLE advertisement uses lower 12 bits: discriminator=0xFFF (4095 mod 4096)
b. Discovery logic crashes on unexpected value
c. Commissioning collision with legitimate device using discriminator=4095
d. Integer overflow if discriminator used in calculations
counterexample_scenario.impact: Discovery protocol violation; potential integer overflow; commissioning collision; non-standard behavior enables fingerprinting attacks

recommendations: Update validate_pake_parameters function signature: validate_pake_parameters(verifier, iterations, salt, discriminator)
Add validation: if (discriminator > 4095) return failure
OR create separate function: validate_commissioning_window_parameters(discriminator, verifier, iterations, salt)
Update T031 guard to use updated validation function
Add error transition T03X: CommissioningWindow_Closed -> ErrorState on discriminator >4095 with appropriate error code
Specification should mandate: 'Receiver SHALL validate discriminator <= 4095 and fail with INVALID_ARGUMENT if exceeded'

attack_path.guard: session_is_CASE == true && fail_safe_armed == true && vid_verification_completed == true
attack_path.violation: No mutual exclusion between state machines
Concurrent operations: commissioning in progress while ACL removal ongoing

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: FSM model contains 4 independent state machines: (1) Joint Commissioning (14 states), (2) ACL Management (5 states), (3) Commissioning Window (5 states), (4) Anchor Transfer (6 states). No guards check for active operations in other state machines. All 4 can run simultaneously without coordination.
race_conditions: Commission + ACL removal = credential installation race
ACL removal + Anchor transfer = datastore contention
Commissioning Window + Joint Commission = simultaneous auth operations

specification_evidence.quote: TransferAnchorStatusDatastoreBusy ... Anchor Transfer was not started due to ongoing Datastore operations
specification_evidence.context: Indicates Anchor Transfer should check for concurrent operations
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Specification acknowledges need for mutual exclusion in at least one operation (Anchor Transfer)

specification_evidence.quote: Busy ... Could not be completed because another commissioning is in progress
specification_evidence.context: Defines Busy status code for concurrent operation detection
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Status code exists for concurrent operations but not consistently enforced

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Busy status code defined; Anchor Transfer checks datastore busy
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: Comprehensive mutual exclusion requirements between operations. Specification doesn't mandate checking for in-progress commissioning before ACL operations, or vice versa. Only Anchor Transfer explicitly mentions datastore busy check.
specification_gap.consequence: Race conditions between concurrent operations on same node; inconsistent state if operations interleave; datastore contention not detected; authority conflicts in anchor transfer during commissioning
specification_gap.security_impact: Credential corruption - ACL removed during commissioning leaves partial access control; Authority confusion - anchor transfer during commissioning creates split-brain; Resource exhaustion - concurrent operations consume unbounded resources; State machine deadlock - operations waiting on each other

steps: 1. Node in Idle state (Joint Commissioning) and ACL_Active (ACL Management)
2. Administrator A sends ICACCSRRequest -> Node enters CSRRequested
3. Node generates CSR -> Node enters CSRGenerated
4. Simultaneously, Administrator B sends RemoveACLFromNode
5. No guard checks: commissioning_in_progress
6. Node accepts and enters ACL_DeletePending (T024)
7. ACL removal starts affecting access control
8. Administrator A sends AddICAC
9. Node validates and installs ICAC (T011)
10. RACE CONDITION:
- ACL removal completes (T027) -> ACL removed from node
- ICAC installation requires ACL for Administrator A
- ACL no longer exists -> Administrator A loses access
- Commissioning succeeds but Administrator locked out
11. Alternative race: Anchor transfer during commissioning
- Transfer starts (T041) -> authority transferred
- Commissioning completes with old anchor authority
- Conflicting certificates issued by old vs new anchor
counterexample_scenario.impact: Credential corruption; authority conflicts; state inconsistency; potential deadlocks; security policy violations; administrator lockout

recommendations: Add global operation lock: Only one administrative operation active at a time
Add guard to all operation entry points: commissioning_in_progress == false
Add guard to ACL operations: operation_ongoing_check() covering commissioning, anchor transfer
Add guard to commissioning: acl_operations_idle() && anchor_transfer_idle()
Implement operation precedence: commissioning > anchor transfer > ACL management
Add mutual exclusion state: OperationInProgress (entered by any admin operation, exited on completion)
Specification should mandate: 'Only one administrative operation (commissioning, ACL management, anchor transfer) may be in progress at any time. Concurrent attempts SHALL be rejected with Busy.'
FSM should use single state machine with sub-states rather than 4 independent state machines

attack_path.violation: No transition to reject concurrent ICACCSRRequest with Busy error

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: FSM accepts ICACCSRRequest only from Idle state (T001). Once commissioning starts (state leaves Idle), subsequent ICACCSRRequest attempts have no defined transitions. FSM implicitly prevents concurrent commissioning through state design but doesn't model explicit Busy rejection. Behavior is undefined: packet dropped? ignored? error returned?
fsm_analysis.missing_transitions: From {CSRRequested, CSRGenerated, ValidatingICAC, ICACAccepted} to ErrorState_Busy on ICACCSRRequest trigger

specification_evidence.quote: Busy ... Could not be completed because another commissioning is in progress
specification_evidence.context: Status code defined for concurrent commissioning
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Status code exists but usage not mandated in ICACCSRRequest Effect on Receipt

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Busy status code defined for concurrent commissioning scenario
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: When and how to use Busy status code. ICACCSRRequest and AddICAC 'Effect on Receipt' sections don't mention checking for in-progress commissioning. Busy is only shown for OpenJointCommissioningWindow.
specification_gap.consequence: Undefined behavior for concurrent ICACCSRRequest; implementation may drop packet, ignore, return wrong error, or crash
specification_gap.security_impact: Denial of service through repeated commissioning attempts; resource exhaustion; state confusion; lack of clear error reporting

steps: 1. Administrator A sends ICACCSRRequest -> Node enters CSRRequested
2. Malicious Actor B sends ICACCSRRequest (concurrent attempt)
3. Node in CSRRequested state receives ICACCSRRequest
4. No transition defined in FSM for this trigger/state combination
5. Implementation-dependent behavior:
a. Ignore packet (silent drop)
b. Process and overwrite existing commissioning state
c. Return INVALID_COMMAND (wrong error)
d. Crash due to unexpected state
6. VIOLATION: Should return Busy but behavior undefined
counterexample_scenario.impact: Interoperability issues - different implementations behave differently; unclear error semantics for clients; potential DoS through concurrent attempts

recommendations: Add transitions: {CSRRequested, CSRGenerated, ValidatingICAC, ICACAccepted} -> ErrorState_Busy on ICACCSRRequest trigger
Guard: commissioning_in_progress == true (implied by being in these states)
Action: send_error_response(Busy, 'Commissioning in progress')
Specification should mandate: 'If ICACCSRRequest received while commissioning in progress, SHALL fail with Busy status code'

attack_path.violation: No size validation before sending CSR; if generate function produces >600 bytes, protocol constraint violated

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: Function generate_PKCS10_CSR_DER_encoded() generates CSR but doesn't check size. Function send_ICACCSRResponse() sends CSR without validating ≤600 bytes. Specification defines 'ICACCSR ... octstr ... max 600' but FSM has no size check.

specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Constraint defined but enforcement not specified

specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: WHERE validation should occur - generator side or receiver side? Should generate function enforce limit? Should send function validate? Should receive function reject oversized?
specification_gap.consequence: Oversized CSR sent if DN fields too long or extensions added; receiver may accept (buffer overflow) or reject (interoperability issue)
specification_gap.security_impact: Buffer overflow on receiver if fixed 600-byte buffer allocated; memory exhaustion if large CSRs repeatedly sent; protocol violation if receiver accepts >600 bytes

steps: 1. Malicious or buggy implementation generates CSR with very long DN fields
2. Distinguished Name: CN=verylongnameexceeding600bytes..., O=longorgname..., OU=longunitname...
3. PKCS#10 CSR DER-encoded size: 650 bytes
4. generate_PKCS10_CSR_DER_encoded() returns 650-byte CSR (no size check)
5. send_ICACCSRResponse(csr) sends 650 bytes (no validation)
6. VIOLATION: CSR exceeds 600-byte limit
7. Receiver behavior:
a. If 600-byte fixed buffer -> buffer overflow
b. If dynamic buffer -> accept oversized CSR (protocol violation)
c. If size validation -> reject with error (interop issue)
counterexample_scenario.impact: Buffer overflow risk on receiver; protocol violation; interoperability issues; potential memory exhaustion DoS

recommendations: Add size validation in generate_PKCS10_CSR_DER_encoded(): if (byte_length(csr) > 600) return error
OR add validation in send_ICACCSRResponse(): if (byte_length(csr) > 600) fail commissioning with error
Update transition T006 guard: add condition csr_size_valid == true
Specification should mandate: 'Generated CSR SHALL not exceed 600 bytes. If exceeded, commissioning SHALL fail.'
Recommended DN field limits: CN ≤ 64 bytes, O ≤ 64 bytes, OU ≤ 64 bytes to ensure CSR fits in 600 bytes

attack_path.violation: No size validation when extracting ICAC; oversized ICAC accepted for validation

attack_path.guard: Crypto_VerifyChain(icac, rcac) == success && validate_public_key_match(icac_pubkey, csr_pubkey) == success && validate_DN_encoding(icac) == success
attack_path.violation: All validation checks pass without checking size; ICAC >400 bytes accepted

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: AddICAC command contains ICACValue field (max 400 bytes per spec). FSM extracts ICAC (T007) and validates: (1) Crypto_VerifyChain, (2) public key match, (3) DN encoding (T011). Size validation is NOT listed as validation check. ICAC >400 bytes would pass all checks and be accepted.

specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Constraint defined but size validation NOT listed in Effect on Receipt validation checks

specification_evidence.quote:
Upon receipt, the ICACValue SHALL be validated in the following ways:
1. Verify the ICAC using `Crypto_VerifyChain`
2. The public key of the ICAC SHALL match
3. The DN Encoding Rules SHALL be validated
specification_evidence.source: Section 11.25.7.3, AddICAC Command, Effect on Receipt
specification_evidence.context: Lists three validation checks - size NOT included
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Size validation missing from explicit validation requirements

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: ICAC field has max 400 bytes constraint
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: Size validation not listed as required check in Effect on Receipt. Only chain, public key, and DN encoding are mandated. Unclear if size is enforced at protocol layer (before FSM) or application layer (in FSM).
specification_gap.consequence: ICAC >400 bytes could be sent by malicious administrator; if receiver doesn't validate size, oversized certificate accepted and stored; memory exhaustion or storage overflow possible
specification_gap.security_impact: Memory exhaustion through repeated oversized ICAC submissions; storage overflow if ICAC persisted; protocol violation if oversized certificates accepted; potential buffer overflow in downstream certificate parser

steps: 1. Malicious Administrator generates ICAC with long DN fields or extensions
2. Certificate size: 450 bytes (exceeds 400-byte limit)
3. Administrator sends AddICAC(ICACValue=450-byte certificate)
4. Node receives and enters ValidatingICAC state (T007)
5. Node performs validations:
- Crypto_VerifyChain(icac, rcac) ✅ passes (certificate properly signed)
- validate_public_key_match(icac_pubkey, csr_pubkey) ✅ passes (keys match)
- validate_DN_encoding(icac) ✅ passes (DN properly encoded)
6. All checks pass, no size validation
7. Node transitions: ValidatingICAC -> ICACAccepted (T011)
8. VIOLATION: 450-byte ICAC accepted despite 400-byte limit
9. Oversized certificate stored and used for authentication
10. Impact: Memory/storage exhaustion if repeated; protocol non-compliance
counterexample_scenario.impact: Memory exhaustion attack; protocol violation; interoperability issues; potential buffer overflow in certificate storage or parsing

recommendations: Add size validation before or during validation sequence
Option 1: Add check in T007 action: if (byte_length(icac) > 400) goto ErrorState
Option 2: Add guard condition in T011-T014: size_valid(icac) == true
Option 3: Add as fourth validation check in ValidatingICAC state
Update specification: Add size validation as 4th check in 'Effect on Receipt': '4. The ICACValue SHALL not exceed 400 bytes. If this check fails, the error status SHALL be InvalidICAC.'
Implementation: Perform size check before cryptographic operations (more efficient)

attack_path.violation: No validation that EndpointID is unique or that endpoint has JFAC cluster

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: Transition T048 accepts AnnounceJointFabricAdministrator with guard=true (unconditional). No validation of EndpointID uniqueness, existence, or JFAC cluster presence. Any endpoint ID accepted and confirmed.

specification_evidence.quote: This field SHALL contain the unique identifier for the endpoint that holds the Joint Fabric Administrator Cluster.
specification_evidence.context: Field definition specifies uniqueness and cluster presence
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Specification defines requirements but doesn't mandate validation on receipt

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: EndpointID SHALL contain unique identifier for endpoint with JFAC cluster
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: No 'Effect on Receipt' section defining validation requirements. Unclear if receiver should validate uniqueness, existence, or cluster presence. Specification defines field semantics but not validation.
specification_gap.consequence: Malicious announcements with invalid endpoints accepted; clients misdirected to wrong endpoint; endpoint without JFAC announced as having JFAC
specification_gap.security_impact: Man-in-the-middle through endpoint redirection; denial of service by announcing non-existent endpoint; confused deputy through announcing endpoint without proper cluster

steps: 1. Attacker (authenticated via CASE) sends AnnounceJointFabricAdministrator(EndpointID=255)
2. Endpoint 255 does not exist on node
3. FSM accepts announcement without validation (T048, guard=true)
4. Node confirms announcement
5. Clients query endpoint 255 for JFAC operations
6. Endpoint doesn't exist -> commands fail
7. Alternative: Endpoint exists but doesn't have JFAC cluster
8. Commands sent to wrong cluster -> wrong behavior
9. Alternative: Attacker announces already-announced endpoint (not unique)
10. Ambiguity: which announcement is authoritative?
counterexample_scenario.impact: DoS through endpoint misdirection; interoperability issues; protocol confusion; potential man-in-the-middle if attacker redirects to malicious endpoint

recommendations: Add validation guard to T048: endpoint_exists(EndpointID) && endpoint_has_JFAC_cluster(EndpointID) && announcement_authorized()
Add error transition: If validation fails, return error status
Specification should add 'Effect on Receipt' section: 'Upon receipt, node SHALL verify: 1. EndpointID exists, 2. Endpoint has JFAC cluster, 3. Announcement authorized'
Track announced endpoints to prevent duplicate announcements (uniqueness)
Optionally: Require digital signature over announcement to prevent spoofing

summary.next_action: Remaining 17 properties (PROP_011-PROP_022, PROP_025, PROP_030, PROP_034, PROP_036-PROP_041, PROP_044) require analysis
summary.confidence_assessment: High confidence in violations found. All violations backed by FSM state transition analysis and specification citations. Some properties marked UNVERIFIABLE due to specification ambiguity or out-of-scope concerns.
